chapters.title: Survival Under Neglect Carries Its Own Logic

summaryBullets: Understanding survival behavior under severe neglect before judging it superficially creates clearer judgment.
People adapt to abandoned conditions in ways outsiders rarely expect.
This matters because judgment improves when survival pressure stays visible in the explanation.
A common mistake is reading extreme adaptation through ordinary middle class assumptions.
The pattern becomes easier to see when behavior looks irrational until the material conditions are put back into the story.
A practical starting move is to restore the context before judging, ask what survival required, and separate adaptation from preference.
